statement: The rewrite should select start handoff patterns per flow using business-keyed workflow IDs and explicit reconciliation states, instead of forcing one universal create/start rule.
rationale: Temporal's start and with-start APIs solve different problems, and the right choice depends on whether the product contract prioritizes an existing Convex row, fused start-and-command semantics, or eventual start after caller death.
keyAssumptions: The team is willing to model and surface reconciliation states such as row_exists_but_workflow_missing
Outbox remains a narrow tool rather than a reintroduced scheduler substrate
validationCriteria: Every create/start flow can be assigned a handoff pattern with explicit repair states
No new flow silently relies on fake Convex+Temporal atomicity
critiquePoints: Overusing outbox patterns could recreate the class of queue complexity the rewrite is trying to delete
questions: Which exact user-facing flows require eventual start even without caller retry?
